===Pokémon===
[[File:Serena Party.png|thumb|250px|Serena's current [[party]]]]
Serena's Pokémon consists of cute and are naturally inclined to Pokémon Performances, such as her {{TP|Serena|Pancham}} and {{TP|Serena|Sylveon}}, who are both great dancers. Also, Serena and her Pokémon are shown to have a tight-knit bond with each other, as she cares for them greatly and likewise. Like {{an|Iris}}, Serena does not have a full party by the end of the series unlike the {{an|Misty|previous}} {{an|May|female}} {{an|Dawn|companions}}. Although she manages to gain her Pokémon's obedience, it took time for Serena to fully utilize {{p|Eevee}} to her potential due to her shyness around others. After the events of ''[[XY105|Party Dancecapades!]]'', Serena is able to manage her team effectively.

Serena frequently cares for her Pokémon's appearance as seen in ''[[XY095|Love Strikes! Eevee, Yikes!]]'', by grooming their coats with her brush. She also {{pkmn|training|trains}} her Pokémon to execute their [[move]]s creatively for their routines in the Freestyle Performances for [[Pokémon Showcase]]s. While Serena does not regularly participate in {{pkmn|battle}}s, her Pokémon are somewhat skilled in battles against other Trainers as seen in ''[[XY090|Tag Team Battle Inspiration!]]''. Her {{TP|Serena|Delphox}} is usually used in battle as her strongest Pokémon, but she has used her in both Pokémon Showcases and Contests.

This listing is of Serena's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}:

|desc=In ''[[M17|Diancie and the Cocoon of Destruction]]'', Serena borrowed a {{p|Rhyhorn}} from the Rhyhorn racing track of [[Orsay City]]. She did so with {{an|Clemont}} and {{an|Bonnie}} to escape with {{an|Diancie}}, who was being chased by thieves. She and Rhyhorn quickly ran past the racers on the track, before going off-track and riding inside a forest. After getting to safety, Serena thanked Rhyhorn for its help and it went back to the racing track.

In ''[[XY053|A Race for Home!]]'', Serena used another Rhyhorn, this time to chase after {{TRT}}.

Baa de Mer Ranch's Rhyhorn's only known move is {{m|Tackle}}{{tt|*|Rhyhorn cannot legally learn Tackle in the games}}.}}

However, it ended up grabbing the attention of one of the Pangoro. After reuniting with Ash's group, {{AP|Fletchinder}} eventually found {{an|Clemont}}, {{AP|Pikachu}}, {{TP|Serena|Braixen}}, and {{TP|Clemont|Bunnelby}}, and Chespin was returned to its trainer.}}
